gurmukhi: ੴ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਵਾਹਿਗੁਰੂ ਜੀ ਕੀ ਫਤਹ ॥
transliteration: ikOankaar satigur prasaadh ||
translation: One, the Formless unfolding as Sound, Victory belongs to the Divine.

gurmukhi: ਪਾਤਸਾਹੀ ੧੦ ॥
transliteration: paatisaahee 10 ||
translation: Tenth Sovereign Guru

gurmukhi: ਕਬਿਯੋ ਬਾਚ ਬੇਨਤੀ ॥
transliteration: kabiyo baach benatee ||
translation: The poet (Guru Gobind Singh) offers this humble prayer.

gurmukhi: ਚੌਪਈ ॥
transliteration: chauapiee ||

gurmukhi: ਹਮਰੀ ਕਰੋ ਹਾਥ ਦੈ ਰੱਛਾ ॥ ਪੂਰਨ ਹੋਇ ਚਿਤ ਕੀ ਇੱਛਾ ॥
transliteration: hamaree karo haath dhai ra'chhaa || pooran hoi chit kee i'chhaa ||
translation: O Divine, give me Your hand and protect me; fulfil this longing within my heart.

gurmukhi: ਤਵ ਚਰਨਨ ਮਨ ਰਹੈ ਹਮਾਰਾ ॥ ਅਪਨਾ ਜਾਨ ਕਰੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਰਾ ॥੩੭੭॥
transliteration: tav charanan man rahai hamaaraa || apanaa jaan karo pratipaaraa ||377||
translation:
O Divine, May my mind remain ever at Your feet.
Recognise me as Your own and take care of me.

gurmukhi: ਹਮਰੇ ਦੁਸਟ ਸਭੈ ਤੁਮ ਘਾਵਹੁ ॥ ਆਪੁ ਹਾਥ ਦੈ ਮੋਹਿ ਬਚਾਵਹੁ ॥
transliteration: hamare dhusaT sabhai tum ghaavahu || aap haath dhai moh bachaavahu ||
translation: Destroy the inner enemies (uncontrolled desire, anger, greed, attachment, arrogance) and with Your own hand, save me.

gurmukhi: ਸੁਖੀ ਬਸੈ ਮੋਰੋ ਪਰਿਵਾਰਾ ॥ ਸੇਵਕ ਸਿੱਖ ਸਭੈ ਕਰਤਾਰਾ ॥੩੭੮॥
transliteration: sukhee basai moro parivaaraa || sevak si'kh sabhai karataaraa ||378||
translation: O Creator, may all my family, those who serve all as You, and those who seek Truth live in inner peace.

gurmukhi: ਮੋ ਰੱਛਾ ਨਿਜ ਕਰ ਦੈ ਕਰਿਯੈ ॥ ਸਭ ਬੈਰਨ ਕੋ ਆਜ ਸੰਘਰਿਯੈ ॥
transliteration: mo ra'chhaa nij kar dhai kariyai || sabh bairan ko aaj sa(n)ghariyai ||
translation: O Divine, protect me with Your own hand; may all inner enemies be destroyed this very day.

gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਨ ਹੋਇ ਹਮਾਰੀ ਆਸਾ ॥ ਤੋਰ ਭਜਨ ਕੀ ਰਹੈ ਪਿਆਸਾ ॥੩੭੯॥
transliteration: pooran hoi hamaaree aasaa || tor bhajan kee rahai piaasaa ||379||
translation: O Divine, fulfil this longing of mine; may the thirst for Your devotion remain alive within me.

gurmukhi: ਤੁਮਹਿ ਛਾਡਿ ਕੋਈ ਅਵਰ ਨ ਧਿਯਾਊਂ ॥ ਜੋ ਬਰ ਚਹੋਂ ਸੁ ਤੁਮ ਤੇ ਪਾਊਂ ॥
transliteration: tumeh chhaadd koiee avar na dhiyaauoo(n) || jo bar chaho(n) su tum te paauoo(n) ||
translation: O Divine, I will not leave You and meditate on anyone else; whatever blessing I seek, I receive only from You.

gurmukhi: ਸੇਵਕ ਸਿੱਖ ਹਮਾਰੇ ਤਾਰੀਅਹਿ ॥ ਚੁਨਿ ਚੁਨਿ ਸਤ੍ਰ ਹਮਾਰੇ ਮਾਰੀਅਹਿ ॥੩੮੦॥
transliteration: sevak si'kh hamaare taare'eh || chun chun satr hamaare maare'eh ||380||
translation: May those who serve and seek the Truth be carried across the ocean of attachment; may our inner enemies be destroyed, one by one.

gurmukhi: ਆਪ ਹਾਥ ਦੈ ਮੁਝੈ ਉਬਰਿਯੈ ॥ ਮਰਨ ਕਾਲ ਕਾ ਤ੍ਰਾਸ ਨਿਵਰਿਯੈ ॥
transliteration: aap haath dhai mujhai ubariyai || maran kaal kaa traas nivariyai ||
translation: O Divine, take me by Your own hand and lift me out; remove the fear of death, time, and change.

gurmukhi: ਹੂਜੋ ਸਦਾ ਹਮਾਰੇ ਪੱਛਾ ॥ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਅਸਿਧੁਜ ਜੂ ਕਰਿਯਹੁ ਰੱਛਾ ॥੩੮੧॥
transliteration: hoojo sadhaa hamaare pa'chhaa || sree asidhuj joo kariyahu ra'chhaa ||381||
translation:
Remain always by our side; O Asidhuj, grant us Your protection.
Footnote: Asidhuj refers to the Divine whose banner bears the sword...a symbol of unwavering discernment, rooted in Divine law, that cuts through illusion.

gurmukhi: ਰਾਖਿ ਲੇਹੁ ਮੁਹਿ ਰਾਖਨਹਾਰੇ ॥ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਸੰਤ ਸਹਾਇ ਪਿਯਾਰੇ ॥
transliteration: raakh leh muh raakhanahaare || saahib sa(n)t sahai piyaare ||
translation: Protect me and keep me, O Divine Protector; Beloved Sovereign, support of the saints.

gurmukhi: ਦੀਨ ਬੰਧੁ ਦੁਸਟਨ ਕੇ ਹੰਤਾ ॥ ਤੁਮ ਹੋ ਪੁਰੀ ਚਤੁਰ ਦਸ ਕੰਤਾ ॥੩੮੨॥
transliteration: dheen ba(n)dh dhusaTan ke ha(n)taa || tum ho puree chatur dhas ka(n)taa ||382||
translation: You are a companion to the humble and the destroyer of ego-driven oppressors; You are sovereign over all fourteen realms.

gurmukhi: ਕਾਲ ਪਾਇ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾ ਬਪੁ ਧਰਾ ॥ ਕਾਲ ਪਾਇ ਸਿਵਜੂ ਅਵਤਰਾ ॥
transliteration: kaal pai brahamaa bap dharaa || kaal pai sivajoo avataraa ||
translation: Through time, Brahma, the creative force, took form; through time, Shiva, the force of transformation, appeared.

gurmukhi: ਕਾਲ ਪਾਇ ਕਰ ਬਿਸਨੁ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਸਾ ॥ ਸਕਲ ਕਾਲ ਕਾ ਕੀਆ ਤਮਾਸਾ ॥੩੮੩
transliteration: kaal pai kar bisan prakaasaa || sakal kaal kaa keeaa tamaasaa ||383||
translation: Through time, Vishnu, the sustaining force, manifested; all of this is the play of time itself.

gurmukhi: ਜਵਨ ਕਾਲ ਜੋਗੀ ਸਿਵ ਕੀਓ ॥ ਬੇਦ ਰਾਜ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾ ਜੂ ਥੀਓ ॥
transliteration: javan kaal jogee siv keeo || bedh raaj brahamaa joo theeo ||
translation: Through time, Shiva became a yogi; through time, Brahma became the knower of the Vedas.

gurmukhi: ਜਵਨ ਕਾਲ ਸਭ ਲੋਕ ਸਵਾਰਾ ॥ ਨਮਸਕਾਰ ਹੈ ਤਾਹਿ ਹਮਾਰਾ ॥੩੮੪॥
transliteration: javan kaal sabh lok savaaraa || namasakaar hai taeh hamaaraa ||384||
translation: Through that same time, all realms were fashioned; my salutation is to the Timeless One.

gurmukhi: ਜਵਨ ਕਾਲ ਸਭ ਜਗਤ ਬਨਾਯੋ ॥ ਦੇਵ ਦੈਤ ਜੱਛਨ ਉਪਜਾਯੋ ॥
transliteration: javan kaal sabh jagat banaayo || dhev dhait ja'chhan upajaayo ||
translation:
Through time, the entire universe was fashioned; gods, demons, and celestial beings arose.
Footnote: Yakshas are subtle celestial beings associated with nature and hidden realms.

gurmukhi: ਆਦਿ ਅੰਤਿ ਏਕੈ ਅਵਤਾਰਾ ॥ ਸੋਈ ਗੁਰੂ ਸਮਝਿਯਹੁ ਹਮਾਰਾ ॥੩੮੫॥
transliteration: aadh a(n)t ekai avataaraa || soiee guroo samajhiyahu hamaaraa ||385||
translation: From beginning to end, there is One manifestation, know That alone as our Guru, the Divine Intelligence.

gurmukhi: ਨਮਸਕਾਰ ਤਿਸ ਹੀ ਕੋ ਹਮਾਰੀ ॥ ਸਕਲ ਪ੍ਰਜਾ ਜਿਨ ਆਪ ਸਵਾਰੀ ॥
transliteration: namasakaar tis hee ko hamaaree || sakal prajaa jin aap savaaree ||
translation: I bow to That One alone, who has fashioned all creation.

gurmukhi: ਸਿਵਕਨ ਕੋ ਸਿਵਗੁਨ ਸੁਖ ਦੀਓ ॥ ਸਤ੍ਰਨ ਕੋ ਪਲ ਮੋ ਬਧ ਕੀਓ ॥੩੮੬॥
transliteration: sivakan ko sivagun sukh dheeo || sata'run ko pal mo badh keeo ||386||
translation:
To the devoted, Divine qualities and peace are given;
ego-driven adversaries are brought to an end in an instant.

gurmukhi: ਘਟ ਘਟ ਕੇ ਅੰਤਰ ਕੀ ਜਾਨਤ ॥ ਭਲੇ ਬੁਰੇ ਕੀ ਪੀਰ ਪਛਾਨਤ ॥
transliteration: ghaT ghaT ke a(n)tar kee jaanat || bhale bure kee peer pachhaanat ||
translation: You know the inner state of every being, recognising the pain beneath intentions we call good and bad.

gurmukhi: ਚੀਟੀ ਤੇ ਕੁੰਚਰ ਅਸਥੂਲਾ ॥ ਸਭ ਪਰ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਕਰ ਫੂਲਾ ॥੩੮੭॥
transliteration: cheeTee te ku(n)char asathoolaa || sabh par kirapaa dhirasaT kar foolaa ||387||
translation: From the ant to the mighty elephant, Your gaze of grace blossoms upon all.

gurmukhi: ਸੰਤਨ ਦੁਖ ਪਾਏ ਤੇ ਦੁਖੀ ॥ ਸੁਖ ਪਾਏ ਸਾਧੁਨ ਕੇ ਸੁਖੀ ॥
transliteration: sa(n)tan dhukh paae te dhukhee || sukh paae saadhun ke sukhee ||
translation: When the saintly suffer, You share in their suffering; when the devoted experience joy, You share in their joy.

gurmukhi: ਏਕ ਏਕ ਕੀ ਪੀਰ ਪਛਾਨੈਂ ॥ ਘਟ ਘਟ ਕੇ ਪਟ ਪਟ ਕੀ ਜਾਨੈਂ ॥੩੮੮॥
transliteration: ek ek kee peer pachhaanai(n) || ghaT ghaT ke paT paT kee jaanai(n) ||388||
translation: You recognise the pain of each and every being, knowing every layer of every heart.

gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਉਦਕਰਖ ਕਰਾ ਕਰਤਾਰਾ ॥ ਪ੍ਰਜਾ ਧਰਤ ਤਬ ਦੇਹ ਅਪਾਰਾ ॥
transliteration: jab udhakarakh karaa karataaraa || prajaa dharat tab dheh apaaraa ||
translation: When the Creative Force expands, countless beings take form.

gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਆਕਰਖ ਕਰਤ ਹੋ ਕਬਹੂੰ ॥ ਤੁਮ ਮੈ ਮਿਲਤ ਦੇਹ ਧਰ ਸਭਹੂੰ ॥੩੮੯॥
transliteration: jab aakarakh karat ho kabahoo(n) || tum mai milat dheh dhar sabhahoo(n) ||389||
translation: When the Creative Force withdraws, all embodied beings merge back into You.

gurmukhi: ਜੇਤੇ ਬਦਨ ਸ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਸਭ ਧਾਰੈ ॥ ਆਪੁ ਆਪਨੀ ਬੂਝ ਉਚਾਰੈ ॥
transliteration: jete badhan sirasaT sabh dhaarai || aap aapanee boojh uchaarai ||
translation: All embodied beings brought forth within creation, each expresses its own understanding.

gurmukhi: ਤੁਮ ਸਭਹੀ ਤੇ ਰਹਤ ਨਿਰਾਲਮ ॥ ਜਾਨਤ ਬੇਦ ਭੇਦ ਅਰ ਆਲਮ ॥੩੯੦॥
transliteration: tum sabhahee te rahat niraalam || jaanat bedh bhedh ar aalam ||390||
translation: You remain untouched by all, knowing the inner distinctions of the Vedas and the world.

gurmukhi: ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ ਨ੍ਰਿਬਿਕਾਰ ਨਿਰਲੰਭ ॥ ਆਦਿ ਅਨੀਲ ਅਨਾਦਿ ਅਸੰਭ ॥
transliteration: nira(n)kaar nirabikaar nirala(n)bh || aadh aneel anaadh asa(n)bh ||
translation: The Divine in essence is formless, without distortion, and beyond grasp; primordial, unstained, without beginning, and uncaused.

gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕਾ ਮੂੜ੍ਹ ਉਚਾਰਤ ਭੇਦਾ ॥ ਜਾ ਕੋ ਭੇਵ ਨ ਪਾਵਤ ਬੇਦਾ ॥੩੯੧॥
transliteration: taa kaa mooRh uchaarat bhedhaa || jaa ko bhev na paavat bedhaa ||391||
translation: Yet the ignorant claim to speak of the Divine’s secrets, secrets that even the Vedas cannot comprehend.

gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕੋ ਕਰਿ ਪਾਹਨ ਅਨੁਮਾਨਤ ॥ ਮਹਾ ਮੂੜ੍ਹ ਕਛੁ ਭੇਦ ਨ ਜਾਨਤ ॥
transliteration: taa ko kar paahan anumaanat || mahaa mooRh kachh bhedh na jaanat ||
translation:
Those who take the Divine to be stone,
in great ignorance, know none of the Divine’s mysteries.

gurmukhi: ਮਹਾਦੇਵ ਕੋ ਕਹਤ ਸਦਾ ਸਿਵ ॥ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ ਕਾ ਚੀਨਤ ਨਹਿ ਭਿਵ ॥੩੯੨॥
transliteration: mahaadhev ko kahat sadhaa siv || nira(n)kaar kaa cheenat neh bhiv ||392||
translation: Some call Mahadev (Shiva, the power of transformation) the eternal One, yet they fail to recognise the Formless Reality.

gurmukhi: ਆਪੁ ਆਪਨੀ ਬੁਧਿ ਹੈ ਜੇਤੀ ॥ ਬਰਨਤ ਭਿੰਨ ਭਿੰਨ ਤੁਹਿ ਤੇਤੀ ॥
transliteration: aap aapanee budh hai jetee || baranat bhi(n)n bhi(n)n tuh tetee ||
translation: Each speaks according to their own capacity of understanding, describing You in different ways accordingly.

gurmukhi: ਤੁਮਰਾ ਲਖਾ ਨ ਜਾਇ ਪਸਾਰਾ ॥ ਕਿਹ ਬਿਧਿ ਸਜਾ ਪ੍ਰਥਮ ਸੰਸਾਰਾ ॥੩੯੩॥
transliteration: tumaraa lakhaa na jai pasaaraa || keh bidh sajaa pratham sa(n)saaraa ||393||
translation: Yet Your vastness cannot be known, nor can one know how the world was first formed.

gurmukhi: ਏਕੈ ਰੂਪ ਅਨੂਪ ਸਰੂਪਾ ॥ ਰੰਕ ਭਯੋ ਰਾਵ ਕਹੀ ਭੂਪਾ ॥
transliteration: ekai roop anoop saroopaa || ra(n)k bhayo raav kahee bhoopaa ||
translation: The One alone is the incomparable form; You become the lowly, and are called king and sovereign.

gurmukhi: ਅੰਡਜ ਜੇਰਜ ਸੇਤਜ ਕੀਨੀ ॥ ਉਤਭੁਜ ਖਾਨਿ ਬਹੁਰ ਰਚਿ ਦੀਨੀ ॥੩੯੪॥
transliteration: a(n)ddaj jeraj setaj keenee || autabhuj khaan bahur rach dheenee ||394||
translation: You manifested Your form through eggs, wombs, and moisture; then You brought forth earth-born life.

gurmukhi: ਕਹੂੰ ਫੂਲ ਰਾਜਾ ਹ੍ਵੈ ਬੈਠਾ ॥ ਕਹੂੰ ਸਿਮਟਿ ਭ੍ਯਿੋ ਸੰਕਰ ਇਕੈਠਾ ॥
transliteration: kahoo(n) fool raajaa havai baiThaa || kahoo(n) simaT bhiyo sa(n)kar ikaiThaa ||
translation:
At times, You appear in full splendour, seated as a king; at times, You gather Yourself together in the form of Shankar.
Footnote: Shankar (Shiva) represents a mode of dissolution and inward concentration within creation; the verse points to appearance, not essence.

gurmukhi: ਸਗਰੀ ਸ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਿਖਾਇ ਅਚੰਭਵ ॥ ਆਦਿ ਜੁਗਾਦਿ ਸਰੂਪ ਸੁਯੰਭਵ ॥੩੯੫॥
transliteration: sagaree sirasaT dhikhai acha(n)bhav || aadh jugaadh saroop suya(n)bhav ||395||
translation: Revealing the whole of creation in wondrous ways, You are the self-existent essence, from the beginning and through all ages.

gurmukhi: ਅਬ ਰੱਛਾ ਮੇਰੀ ਤੁਮ ਕਰੋ ॥ ਸਿੱਖ ਉਬਾਰਿ ਅਸਿੱਖ ਸੰਘਰੋ ॥
transliteration: ab ra'chhaa meree tum karo || si'kh ubaar asi'kh sa(n)gharo ||
translation: Now grant me Your protection. Uplift and save those who seek Truth, and destroy the forces that oppose it.

gurmukhi: ਦੁਸ਼ਟ ਜਿਤੇ ਉਠਵਤ ਉਤਪਾਤਾ ॥ ਸਕਲ ਮਲੇਛ ਕਰੋ ਰਣ ਘਾਤਾ ॥੩੯੬॥
transliteration: dhushaT jite uThavat utapaataa || sakal malechh karo ran ghaataa ||396||
translation: Wherever corrupt forces rise to create chaos, defeat all destructive forces on the battlefield.

gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਅਸਿਧੁਜ ਤਵ ਸਰਨੀ ਪਰੇ ॥ ਤਿਨ ਕੇ ਦੁਸ਼ਟ ਦੁਖਿਤ ਹ੍ਵੈ ਮਰੇ ॥
transliteration: je asidhuj tav saranee pare || tin ke dhushaT dhukhit havai mare ||
translation: Those who take sanctuary in Your presence, O Asidhuj, the corrupt forces opposing them are afflicted and brought to an end.

gurmukhi: ਪੁਰਖ ਜਵਨ ਪਗ ਪਰੇ ਤਿਹਾਰੇ ॥ ਤਿਨ ਕੇ ਤੁਮ ਸੰਕਟ ਸਭ ਟਾਰੇ ॥੩੯੭॥
transliteration: purakh javan pag pare tihaare || tin ke tum sa(n)kaT sabh Taare ||397||
translation: Those who fall at Your feet, You remove all their difficulties.

gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਕਲਿ ਕੋ ਇਕ ਬਾਰ ਧਿਐ ਹੈ ॥ ਤਾ ਕੇ ਕਾਲ ਨਿਕਟਿ ਨਹਿ ਐਹੈ ॥
transliteration: jo kal ko ik baar dhiaai hai || taa ke kaal nikaT neh aaihai ||
translation: Whoever remembers the Divine even once, death and time do not come near them.

gurmukhi: ਰੱਛਾ ਹੋਇ ਤਾਹਿ ਸਭ ਕਾਲਾ ॥ ਦੁਸਟ ਅਰਿਸਟ ਟਰੇਂ ਤਤਕਾਲਾ ॥੩੯੮॥
transliteration: ra'chhaa hoi taeh sabh kaalaa || dhusaT arisaT Tare(n) tatakaalaa ||398||
translation: They remain protected at all times; inner enemies and obstacles are removed immediately.

gurmukhi: ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਤਨ ਜਾਹਿ ਨਿਹਰਿਹੋ ॥ ਤਾਕੇ ਤਾਪ ਤਨਕ ਮੋ ਹਰਿਹੋ ॥
transliteration: kirapaa dhirasaT tan jaeh nihariho || taake taap tanak mo hariho ||
translation: Upon whom Your gaze of grace falls, their suffering is removed instantly.

gurmukhi: ਰਿੱਧਿ ਸਿੱਧਿ ਘਰ ਮੋ ਸਭ ਹੋਈ ॥ ਦੁਸ਼ਟ ਛਾਹ ਛ੍ਵੈ ਸਕੈ ਨ ਕੋਈ ॥੩੯੯॥
transliteration: ri'dh si'dh ghar mo sabh hoiee || dhushaT chhaeh chhavai sakai na koiee ||399||
translation: Abundance and worldly attainments dwell within; no inner enemy can even touch their shadow.

gurmukhi: ਏਕ ਬਾਰ ਜਿਨ ਤੁਮੈ ਸੰਭਾਰਾ ॥ ਕਾਲ ਫਾਸ ਤੇ ਤਾਹਿ ਉਬਾਰਾ ॥
transliteration: ek baar jin tumai sa(n)bhaaraa || kaal faas te taeh ubaaraa ||
translation: Those who remember You even once are freed from the noose of time and death.

gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਨਰ ਨਾਮ ਤਿਹਾਰੋ ਕਹਾ ॥ ਦਾਰਿਦ ਦੁਸਟ ਦੋਖ ਤੇ ਰਹਾ ॥੪੦੦॥
transliteration: jin nar naam tihaaro kahaa || dhaaridh dhusaT dhokh te rahaa ||400||
translation: Those who utter Your Name remain free from poverty, inner enemies, and faults.

gurmukhi: ਖੜਗ ਕੇਤ ਮੈ ਸਰਣਿ ਤਿਹਾਰੀ ॥ ਆਪ ਹਾਥ ਦੈ ਲੇਹੁ ਉਬਾਰੀ ॥
transliteration: khaRag ket mai saran tihaaree || aap haath dhai leh ubaaree ||
translation: O Bearer of the Sword, I take Your sanctuary; take me by Your own hand and lift me out.

gurmukhi: ਸਰਬ ਠੌਰ ਮੋ ਹੋਹੁ ਸਹਾਈ ॥ ਦੁਸਟ ਦੋਖ ਤੇ ਲੇਹੁ ਬਚਾਈ ॥੪੦੧॥
transliteration: sarab Thauar mo hoh sahaiee || dhusaT dhokh te leh bachaiee ||401||
translation: Be my support in all places and at all times; protect me from inner enemies and faults.

gurmukhi: ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕਰੀ ਹਮ ਪਰ ਜਗਮਾਤਾ ॥ ਗ੍ਰੰਥ ਕਰਾ ਪੂਰਨ ਸੁਭਰਾਤਾ ॥
transliteration: kirapaa karee ham par jagamaataa || gra(n)th karaa pooran subharaataa ||
translation: Grace has been bestowed upon me, O Mother of the world; this composition has been completed, filled with divine qualities.

gurmukhi: ਕਿਲਬਿਖ ਸਕਲ ਦੇਹ ਕੋ ਹਰਤਾ ॥ ਦੁਸਟ ਦੋਖਿਯਨ ਕੋ ਛੈ ਕਰਤਾ ॥੪੦੨॥
transliteration: kilabikh sakal dheh ko harataa || dhusaT dhokhiyan ko chhai karataa ||402||
translation: You remove all burdens from the being, and bring inner enemies and faults to an end.

gurmukhi: ਸ੍ਰੀ ਅਸਿਧੁਜ ਜਬ ਭਏ ਦਯਾਲਾ ॥ ਪੂਰਨ ਕਰਾ ਗ੍ਰੰਥ ਤਤਕਾਲਾ ॥
transliteration: sree asidhuj jab bhe dhayaalaa || pooran karaa gra(n)th tatakaalaa ||
translation: When Sri Asidhuj became compassionate, this composition was completed immediately.

gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਬਾਛਤ ਫਲ ਪਾਵੈ ਸੋਈ ॥ ਦੂਖ ਨ ਤਿਸੈ ਬਿਆਪਤ ਕੋਈ ॥੪੦੩॥
transliteration: man baachhat fal paavai soiee || dhookh na tisai biaapat koiee ||403||
translation: Whoever recites it receives the fruits their heart longs for; no suffering overcomes them.

gurmukhi: ਅੜਿਲ ॥
transliteration: aRil ||
translation: AAril

gurmukhi: ਸੁਨੈ ਗੁੰਗ ਜੋ ਯਾਹਿ ਸੁ ਰਸਨਾ ਪਾਵਈ ॥
transliteration: sunai gu(n)g jo yaeh su rasanaa paaviee ||
translation: One who is mute to Truth, upon listening to this composition, receives the capacity for truthful and meaningful expression.

gurmukhi: ਸੁਨੈ ਮੂੜ ਚਿਤ ਲਾਇ ਚਤੁਰਤਾ ਆਵਈ ॥
transliteration: sunai mooR chit lai chaturataa aaviee ||
translation: Even the ignorant, when they listen attentively, awaken to wisdom.

gurmukhi: ਦੂਖ ਦਰਦ ਭੌ ਨਿਕਟ ਨ ਤਿਨ ਨਰ ਕੇ ਰਹੈ ॥ ਹੋ ਜੋ ਯਾ ਕੀ ਏਕ ਬਾਰ ਚੌਪਈ ਕੋ ਕਹੈ ॥੪੦੪॥
transliteration: dhookh dharadh bhau nikaT na tin nar ke rahai || ho jo yaa kee ek baar chauapiee ko kahai ||404||
translation: Suffering, pain, and fear do not come near one who recites this Chaupai even once.

gurmukhi: ਸ੍ਵੈਯਾਪਾਇ ਗਹੇ ਜਬ ਤੇ ਤੁਮਰੇ ਤਬ ਤੇ ਕੋਊ ਆਂਖ ਤਰੇ ਨਹੀ ਆਨਯੋ ॥
transliteration: pai gahe jab te tumare tab te kouoo aa(n)kh tare nahee aanayo ||
translation: Since I have held onto Your feet, my gaze has not turned toward any other. O Bearer of the Sword, by Your grace, nothing I have spoken is mine — it is all Your expression.
